title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Go Semantics Case: Single Reader Writer Ownership
summary: A parser is recursive and side-effectful, multiple goroutines read one connection, cancellation races with response writes, and error paths cannot state which component owns the buffer or close. Planned artifact: Add and verify the single reader writer ownership increment in explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.
Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Single Reader Writer Ownership · Theory Single Reader Writer Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1703 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Single Reader Writer Ownership contract for the supplied data.
